method: Visible screenshot only; ordinary merchant language; no source, logs, APIs, hidden state, or implementation history.
screen_journey_job: Help the seller understand and act on this paid order now—dispatch, fulfill, or refund as offered.
first_friction: Before the seller can focus on “Dispatch order,” the top bar already asks them to parse assistant permissions, a green “Assistant status: Ready,” and a green “Identity status: Verified”—healthy setup noise that does not change what to do with this order.

findings_prioritized.title: Healthy status pills crowd the order workspace
findings_prioritized.detail: “Assistant status: Ready” and “Identity status: Verified” sit in the persistent header on an order that already shows Payment: Paid and a clear Dispatch action. For a shopkeeper, green “all good” badges are not a reason to look up; they compete with the job. Keep them out of the bar unless something is blocked or needs approval, and then put that warning beside the affected action.

findings_prioritized.title: Assistant permissions is treated like a main shop task
findings_prioritized.detail: “Assistant permissions” appears as a first-class nav item next to Dashboard, Catalog, and Orders, and the brand subtitle even advertises it. Permissions are account/setup, not a daily order path. A merchant opening a paid order should not be offered permissions as if it were peer to Orders.

findings_prioritized.title: Header mixes shop navigation with account chrome
findings_prioritized.detail: Brand, primary nav, Search, two status pills, and Sign out all share one strip. Cross-page shop moves (Dashboard / Catalog / Orders / Settings) belong in persistent nav; identity detail, assistant health, and sign-out belong under an account or Settings grouping so the order page stays about the order.

findings_prioritized.title: Brand line sells setup, not the store
findings_prioritized.detail: Subtitle “Catalog, orders, and assistant permissions” reads like a product tour, not a store name. On order detail it reinforces assistant/setup over “what do I do with this paid order?”

findings_prioritized.title: Body job is clearer than the chrome
findings_prioritized.detail: Buyer/delivery, Payment: Paid, Dispatch order, Review full refund, and line items are understandable. Samantha as a corner FAB is a reasonable on-demand help entry. The fail is admission at the top, not the order cards themselves.

brand.note: Keep product/store identity in the header. Drop or shorten the setup-flavored subtitle on operational pages.

primary_nav_dashboard_catalog_orders_set.note: Frequent cross-page shop tasks; Orders correctly marked active. Settings is the right home for account/setup items moved out of the bar.

assistant_permissions.note: Not a daily order task. Move into Settings or account grouping; do not keep as peer primary nav.

search.note: Useful across lists; on a single order detail it is secondary. Acceptable as a compact global control if used often to jump orders/catalog; otherwise surface from Orders/Catalog lists.

assistant_status_ready.note: Healthy “Ready” is not consequential changing state for this screen. Prefer On demand inside account/assistant area; if degraded/blocked, show Interruptive or Contextual beside the assistant or the blocked action—not a permanent green pill.

identity_status_verified.note: Healthy “Verified” belongs under account/identity, not persistent header. Surface only when unverified, expired, or blocking a seller action—beside that action.

sign_out.note: Conventional logged-in control, but not needed as a always-visible text link next to status pills. Place under account menu; keep one-click reachable.

samantha_fab.note: Appropriate progressive disclosure for help/assistant without owning the order decision. Keep out of the persistent header strip.

set: Limited primary nav if public (e.g. marketing or read-only home)—or none
exclude: Account menu contents that assume a session
recommended_logged_out_header.convention_note: For this product class, logged-out chrome admits brand + entry to sign in. Logged-in chrome admits brand + shop nav + account entry; identity/assistant health live inside account unless a degraded state blocks work.

beside_task_when_needed: Degraded assistant or identity that blocks dispatch/refund
Approval or payment blockers next to the affected button

header_admission_summary: Keep in persistent header only frequent cross-page shop tasks, changing consequential state, or conventional global controls. Move healthy status, identity detail, permissions, and connection config into account/Settings. Surface blocked/approval states beside the affected order action.
unfinished_items: Logged-out header not shown in this screenshot—recommendation is by product-class convention only.
Did not exercise Search, Assistant permissions, Samantha, Sign out, or Dispatch—judgment is visual/admission only.
word_budget_note: Report kept concise for ~6 minute review; parent summary separate.
